name: failure
description: Failure semantics and durability: the structured step-result contract shared by every consumer (threading, journal, metering, reporting), fail-stop with declared on_failure cleanup hooks, opt-in retry with between-attempt cleanup, the run journal keyed (step-id, input-hash), and the resume/fresh/interactive recovery modes.

requirements.title: Structured step result
requirements.description: Every step emits exactly one result record: {status: ok|failed, payload (schema-typed) | error {reason, detail, handoff}, timing, attempt}. Failure is a record, not an absence. An unfavorable-but-valid payload (a review verdict of 'changes') is status ok — workflow conditions, not failure semantics, react to it.

requirements.title: Fail-stop and cleanup hooks
requirements.description: A failed step halts its dependency chain (fail-stop); independent branches continue. A template or step may declare an on_failure hook — an opaque user script faber runs host-side (or in a fresh box) with the failed step's inputs and error record, to undo external side-effects (delete an orphan branch, release a claimed item). Cleanup failure is reported but does not mask the original failure.

requirements.title: Opt-in retry with cleanup
requirements.description: retry: N on a step re-runs the whole step (a step is atomic — never resume into an agent's chain of thought). on_failure cleanup runs between attempts, which is what guarantees each attempt starts from a clean slate. Exhausted retries yield the final failure record with attempt history.

requirements.title: Run journal
requirements.description: Every completed step's result is journaled under the key (step-id, input-hash), where input-hash covers the step's resolved inputs, template identity, and image tag. The journal is the run's durable state: append-only records sufficient to reconstruct scheduler state and reuse prior outputs.

requirements.title: Recovery modes
requirements.description: Three ways to re-enter a failed run: resume (journal hits with matching input-hash are skipped, execution restarts at the first failed or absent step), fresh (--fresh: ignore the journal, run everything), and interactive (drop the operator into the failed step's box — same image, bindings, and inputs — for manual diagnosis). A failed generate item is a normal failure plus journal entry, not a special partial-run state.

requirements.title: Deferred: timeouts and cancellation
requirements.description: Backlog (design edge case 6): per-step wall-clock timeouts and user-initiated abort with in-flight container cleanup (kill, teardown of bindings, journal record of the interruption). First pass has no step timeout; abort is process-level.

requirements.title: Deferred: journal store concurrency
requirements.description: Backlog (design edge case 10): the journal store's canonical location and multi-run concurrency — two runs claiming the same generate item need dedup or locking. First pass: one journal directory per run, no cross-run coordination.

components.name: ResultContract
components.description: The step-result record type and its invariants: status vocabulary, schema-typed payload, error record with handoff pointer, timing and attempt metadata. The single contract consumed by threading, journal, metering, and reporting.

components.name: FailurePolicy
components.description: Fail-stop semantics, on_failure hook execution (host-side opaque script with the error record), retry orchestration with between-attempt cleanup, and attempt accounting. Owns the deferred timeout/cancellation seam.

components.name: Journal
components.description: The append-only run journal: (step-id, input-hash) keying, input-hash computation, record encoding, lookup for resume decisions, per-step actual-cost records, the run-end marker, and same-run process exclusivity (the advisory run lock). Owns the on-disk journal schema version (format: 1) — bumped only when record shapes change; replay fails closed on any other stamp, no auto-migration. Owns the deferred store-location/cross-run-concurrency seam.

components.name: RecoveryModes
components.description: The resume/fresh/interactive entry points: journal replay into scheduler state, fail-closed version and drift guards (journal format, IR version, IR hash, params, recorded image tags), cache-bypass, the interactive re-entry that reconstructs the failed step's box (journaled image tag, bindings, slot-keyed handoff inputs) for an operator shell, and the read-only faber upgrade-check run audit.

data_flows.name: Failure and resume flow
data_flows.description: Step failure record -> on_failure cleanup -> retry decision (attempts left?) -> journal append -> fail-stop propagation; on re-entry: journal lookup by (step-id, input-hash) -> skip / re-run / interactive shell.
